title: Continuation: network-diagnose hardening + narrow worker-only recovery workflow

sha_binding.verified_source_note: Continuation of PR #1258, frozen at the T1 bounce limit (4 CHANGES_REQUIRED verdicts) per PM direction. Same already-reviewed content carried forward on a fresh branch from current main.

verifier.note: Mechanical checks only. Requires t1-approved label and a Griff-authored pm-verdict/v1 APPROVED comment bound to the exact head before merge.

r_level.command: pnpm exec tsx scripts/ci/r-level-check.ts --base origin/main --head HEAD

workflow_safety_review.note: Same read-only/no-mutation safety review already passed across #1258's review rounds, unchanged in this continuation:

checks.check: Only SSH commands executed are read-only: getent, dig, curl, openssl s_client, docker network ls/inspect, docker logs, ip route/link, sudo iptables -L / ufw status (best-effort)
No docker restart/stop/rm/exec-with-write, no sed/tee/> against .env.production or any config file, no queue/DB write commands
Secret values are never echoed -- only hostnames/ports parsed out of URL-style values, DB test is a bare TCP connect with no credentials sent
ops-worker-recovery.yml is workflow_dispatch-only -- no automatic/scheduled execution, no trigger on push/PR events, this PR does not dispatch it

runtime_proof.note: Live smoke run per T1 policy; this lane changes only GitHub Actions workflow files, a test file, and package.json's test list -- no production code path or migration.

acceptance_criteria.network_diagnose_hardening_carried_forwa: PASS -- DB/pooler key discovery + curl-or-node HTTPS fallback

known_gaps: Docker daemon events window covers only 7 days.
Deploy-timestamp correlation done at the analysis layer, not inside the SSH probe.
